reasoning: The EXPINF10YR series exhibits behavior close to a random walk with minor fluctuations, currently sitting around 2.3%. The FRED linear model predicts a slight positive drift with a baseline 55% probability of increase in the near term. Recent readings have been flat to slightly up. Given the structural nature of long-term inflation expectations, movements are typically gradual. As the forecast horizon increases, uncertainty grows, meaning the probability of the series being strictly higher than the reference value on May 11, 2025 should regress toward 50%. Therefore, I start near the model estimate (54%) for the closest horizon (June 10) and degrade it toward 50% for the further resolution dates (52% for Aug 9, and 51% for Nov 7).
